title: Creamy Smoky Indian Potato Hash
description: A smoky and silky post-workout dish featuring floury potatoes, this recipe is an ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly polish dish cooked to perfection in an iron teppan, providing a delicious and satisfying meal after a workout.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan over medium heat and add the olive oil to heat for 1 minute.
Add the sliced onion to the teppan and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the teppan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the diced potatoes to the teppan, stirring to combine with the onion and garlic mixture, and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the smoked paprika and garam masala to the teppan, stirring to combine, and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the yogurt and cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the potatoes are coated in the creamy sauce.
Season the potato hash with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish the potato hash with chopped cilantro and serve hot.
Cook the potato hash for an additional 2 minutes, if desired, to crisp the bottom.
Remove the potato hash from the teppan and serve immediately.
